skill.name: making-commits
skill.description: Guidelines for making small, focused, and maintainable git commits.
skill.summary: Helps contributors plan clean commit history for larger development tasks.
skill.author: Crossbill-Highlights

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line is only front matter description text for git commit guidance, with no cryptographic operation, code execution, network access, or prompt injection content found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3, but the line contains only the skill description text. No hash function, cipher, credential handling, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain YAML front matter text. The file contains only commit hygiene guidance and no code or crypto-related behavior.

content.value_statement: Large development tasks often produce unclear commit history. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create small commits, separate concerns, and run formatting checks first.
actual_capabilities: Encourages small commits that address one concern or feature.
Guides agents to split larger changes into logical commit groups.
Reminds agents to run linting before committing.
Reminds agents to run formatting before committing.
Supports cleaner review history for larger coding tasks.
limitations: Does not define exact commit message formatting rules.
Does not run git commands by itself.
Does not choose project-specific lint or formatting commands.
Does not replace human review of staged changes.

use_cases.title: Split Feature Work
use_cases.description: Break a broad feature into focused commits that reviewers can inspect one at a time.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Changes
use_cases.description: Organize a messy worktree into commits with clear boundaries before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Commits
use_cases.description: Give AI coding agents a simple rule set for maintainable commit history.

prompt_templates.title: Create Focused Commits
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current changes and suggest a small set of focused git commits. Keep each commit limited to one concern.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for cleaning up local changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Commit Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Before committing, group these changes into logical commit boundaries. Explain which files belong together and why.

prompt_templates.title: Check Commit Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my changes are ready to commit. Identify linting, formatting, and review issues I should resolve first.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Patch Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me turn this completed task into a clean patch series with focused commits and a maintainable review order.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for complex pull requests.

output_examples.input: I changed authentication logic, updated tests, and formatted unrelated files.
output: Commit 3: Formatting-only changes, if they are required.

output_examples.input: I need to know whether this work is ready to commit.
output_examples.output: The changes should be linted and formatted first. Then split them by concern so each commit is easy to review.

output_examples.input: My feature took several days and touched many files.
output_examples.output: Create commits in logical order: shared types first, implementation second, tests third, and cleanup last.

best_practices: Review staged changes before each commit.
Keep each commit focused on one feature, fix, or cleanup.
Run the project lint and formatting commands before committing.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated fixes in one commit.
Do not commit large unreviewed changes without splitting them.
Do not skip formatting checks before committing.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents create clean and maintainable git commit history during larger coding tasks.

faq.question: Does it run git commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides commit workflow guidance but does not execute git commands by itself.

faq.question: Can it write commit messages?
faq.answer: It can help plan commit boundaries, but the skill does not define a detailed commit message format.

faq.question: Should I still review the changes?
faq.answer: Yes. You should inspect staged changes and confirm each commit has the intended scope.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains only commit workflow guidance and no executable behavior.
